2603.22665.title: Improving LLM Predictions via Inter-Layer Structural Encoders
2603.22665.paper_summary: The standard practice in Large Language Models (LLMs) is to base predictions on the final-layer token representations. Recent studies, however, show that intermediate layers encode substantial information, which may contain more task-relevant features than the final-layer representations alone. Importantly, it was shown that for different tasks, different layers may be optimal. In this work we introduce Inter-Layer Structural Encoders (ILSE), a powerful structural approach to learn one effective representation from the LLM's internal layer representations all together. Central to ILSE is Cayley-Encoder, a mathematically grounded geometric encoder that leverages expander Cayley graphs for efficient inter-layer information propagation. We evaluate ILSE across 13 classification and semantic similarity tasks with 9 pre-trained LLMs ranging from 14 million to 8 billion parameters. ILSE consistently outperforms baselines and existing approaches, achieving up to 44% improvement in accuracy and 25% in similarity metrics. We further show that ILSE is data-efficient in few-shot regimes and can make small LLMs competitive with substantially larger models.
2603.22665.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的標準做法是基於最終層的標記表示來進行預測。然而，最近的研究顯示，中間層編碼了大量信息，這些信息可能包含比最終層表示更相關的任務特徵。重要的是，已經顯示對於不同的任務，不同的層可能是最佳的。在這項工作中，我們介紹了層間結構編碼器（ILSE），這是一種強大的結構方法，可以從LLM的內部層表示中學習一個有效的表示。ILSE的核心是Cayley-Encoder，這是一種數學上有根據的幾何編碼器，利用擴展的Cayley圖進行高效的層間信息傳播。我們在13個分類和語義相似性任務中評估ILSE，使用了9個從1400萬到80億參數的預訓練LLM。ILSE始終超越基準和現有方法，在準確性上提高了最多44%，在相似性指標上提高了25%。我們進一步顯示ILSE在少量樣本的情況下數據效率高，並且可以使小型LLM與大得多的模型具備競爭力。
2603.22665.author: Tom Ulanovski et.al.
2603.22665.authors: Tom Ulanovski, Eyal Blyachman, Maya Bechler-Speicher
